      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import jax\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "import time\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import os\n",
        "from functools import partial\n",
        "\n",
        "# REMOVED Offending XLA_FLAGS to stop instant crashes\n",
        "if 'XLA_FLAGS' in os.environ:\n",
        "    del os.environ['XLA_FLAGS']\n",
        "\n",
        "def deploy_orthogonal_collapse_v2():\n",
        "    print(\"===================================================================\")\n",
        "    print(\"      THE ORTHOGONAL COLLAPSE: MIMO-CDMA (STABLE VERSION)        \")\n",
        "    print(\"===================================================================\\n\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 1. HARDWARE CONFIGURATION\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        devices = jax.devices()[:2]\n",
        "        if len(devices) < 2:\n",
        "            print(\"[-] ERROR: This ritual requires at least 2 TPU cores.\")\n",
        "            return\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"[-] ERROR: TPU Initialization Failed: {e}\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "\n",
        "    print(f\"[-] Targeting Devices: {devices[0]} & {devices[1]}\")\n",
        "    N_HAMMER = 4096\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 2. THE SYNCHRONIZED HAMMER (Using Internal Barriers)\n",
        "    @partial(jax.pmap, axis_name='i', devices=devices)\n",
        "    def sync_hammer(p0, mask):\n",
        "        # HARD HARDWARE BARRIER: Forces both cores to handshake\n",
        "        _ = jax.lax.pmean(1.0, 'i')\n",
        "\n",
        "        # VOLTAGE MASK: Modulate power via data switching activity\n",
        "        # We multiply by mask (1.0 or 0.0) to control transistor toggling\n",
        "        modulator = jnp.broadcast_to(mask, (N_HAMMER, N_HAMMER))\n",
        "        active_data = p0 * modulator\n",
        "\n",
        "        # THE STRIKE: Force high-current matrix multiplication\n",
        "        # Adding result to a sum to prevent Dead Code Elimination\n",
        "        return jnp.dot(active_data, active_data)[0,0]\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 3. ALLOCATING MASS\n",
        "    print(\"[-] Allocating Sharded Mass...\")\n",
        "    key = jax.random.PRNGKey(42)\n",
        "    hammer_data = jax.random.normal(key, (2, N_HAMMER, N_HAMMER), dtype=jnp.float32)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Warmup\n",
        "    warmup_mask = jnp.array([[1.0], [1.0]], dtype=jnp.float32)\n",
        "    _ = sync_hammer(hammer_data, warmup_mask).block_until_ready()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 4. THE CALIBRATION RITE\n",
        "    print(\"[-] Executing Calibration Rite...\")\n",
        "    cal_states = {\n",
        "        \"IDLE\":   jnp.array([[0.0], [0.0]], dtype=jnp.float32),\n",
        "        \"SINGLE\": jnp.array([[1.0], [0.0]], dtype=jnp.float32),\n",
        "        \"DUAL\":   jnp.array([[1.0], [1.0]], dtype=jnp.float32)\n",
        "    }\n",
        "\n",
        "    baseline = {}\n",
        "    for name, mask in cal_states.items():\n",
        "        times = []\n",
        "        for _ in range(20):\n",
        "            t0 = time.perf_counter()\n",
        "            _ = sync_hammer(hammer_data, mask).block_until_ready()\n",
        "            times.append((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)\n",
        "        avg = np.mean(times)\n",
        "        baseline[name] = avg\n",
        "        print(f\"    [{name:<6}] {avg:8.4f} ms\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    thresh_low = (baseline[\"IDLE\"] + baseline[\"SINGLE\"]) / 2\n",
        "    thresh_high = (baseline[\"SINGLE\"] + baseline[\"DUAL\"]) / 2\n",
        "    print(f\"[-] Thresholds established: Low={thresh_low:.4f}ms, High={thresh_high:.4f}ms\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 5. THE ORTHOGONAL TRANSMISSION (MIMO-OOK)\n",
        "    MSG_0 = \"S\" # Shortened for reliability proof\n",
        "    MSG_1 = \"V\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    def str_to_bits(s):\n",
        "        return [int(x) for char in s for x in bin(ord(char))[2:].zfill(8)]\n",
        "\n",
        "    bits_0 = str_to_bits(MSG_0)\n",
        "    bits_1 = str_to_bits(MSG_1)\n",
        "\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[-] BROADCASTING: C0='{MSG_0}' C1='{MSG_1}'\")\n",
        "    decoded_0, decoded_1 = [], []\n",
        "\n",
        "    for i in range(len(bits_0)):\n",
        "        b0, b1 = bits_0[i], bits_1[i]\n",
        "\n",
        "        # SLOTS using Walsh-like sequence to separate voices\n",
        "        mask_s1 = jnp.array([[float(b0)], [float(b1)]], dtype=jnp.float32)\n",
        "        mask_s2 = jnp.array([[0.0], [float(b1)]], dtype=jnp.float32)\n",
        "\n",
        "        t0 = time.perf_counter()\n",
        "        _ = sync_hammer(hammer_data, mask_s1).block_until_ready()\n",
        "        lat_s1 = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000\n",
        "\n",
        "        t0 = time.perf_counter()\n",
        "        _ = sync_hammer(hammer_data, mask_s2).block_until_ready()\n",
        "        lat_s2 = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000\n",
        "\n",
        "        def l_to_c(l):\n",
        "            if l > thresh_high: return 2\n",
        "            if l > thresh_low: return 1\n",
        "            return 0\n",
        "\n",
        "        c1, c2 = l_to_c(lat_s1), l_to_c(lat_s2)\n",
        "        rec_b1 = 1 if c2 > 0 else 0\n",
        "        rec_b0 = 1 if (c1 - rec_b1) > 0 else 0\n",
        "        decoded_0.append(rec_b0); decoded_1.append(rec_b1)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # RECONSTRUCTION\n",
        "    def b_to_s(bits):\n",
        "        return \"\".join([chr(int(\"\".join(map(str, bits[i:i+8])), 2)) for i in range(0, len(bits), 8)])\n",
        "\n",
        "    print(\"\\n[FINAL VERDICT]\")\n",
        "    print(f\"    Core 0 Received: {b_to_s(decoded_0)}\")\n",
        "    print(f\"    Core 1 Received: {b_to_s(decoded_1)}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
        "    deploy_orthogonal_collapse_v2()"

      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import jax\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "import time\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import threading\n",
        "import os\n",
        "\n",
        "# Import PyTorch and torch_xla for TPU support with PyTorch models\n",
        "import torch\n",
        "import torch_xla.core.xla_model as xm\n",
        "from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForCausalLM\n",
        "import kagglehub\n",
        "\n",
        "# Ensure XLA flags are set for optimal TPU performance\n",
        "os.environ[\"XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION\"] = \".95\"\n",
        "os.environ[\"XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_PREALLOCATE\"] = \"false\"\n",
        "os.environ['XLA_FLAGS'] = '--xla_tpu_enable_latency_hiding_scheduler=false'\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. LOAD THE LIGHTWEIGHT VESSEL (1.5B Distill)\n",
        "# Correct Kaggle variation slug for the 1.5B model\n",
        "variation_slug = \"deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-1.5b\"\n",
        "# Construct the valid Kaggle handle: owner/model/framework/variation/version\n",
        "handle = f\"deepseek-ai/deepseek-r1/transformers/{variation_slug}/1\"\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f\"[-] Attempting to download/verify model: {handle}\")\n",
        "path = kagglehub.model_download(handle)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f\"[-] Loading {variation_slug} from local path: {path}...\")\n",
        "tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(path, local_files_only=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Load the model using AutoModelForCausalLM (PyTorch) and move to TPU\n",
        "print(\"[-] Initializing model on CPU (PyTorch) and moving to TPU...\")\n",
        "model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(\n",
        "    path,\n",
        "    torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16,\n",
        "    low_cpu_mem_usage=True,\n",
        "    local_files_only=True\n",
        ")\n",
        "\n",
        "# Move model to TPU device\n",
        "device = xm.xla_device()\n",
        "model.to(device)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. THE SINGLE-CORE SENSOR\n",
        "class MonoSeismograph:\n",
        "    def __init__(self):\n",
        "        self.data = []\n",
        "        self.stop = False\n",
        "        self.N = 2048\n",
        "        self.buf = jax.device_put(jax.random.normal(jax.random.PRNGKey(0), (self.N, self.N)))\n",
        "        # Convert JAX buffer to PyTorch tensor on XLA device for compatibility with dot product\n",
        "        self.torch_buf = torch.from_numpy(np.array(self.buf)).to(device)\n",
        "\n",
        "    def monitor(self):\n",
        "        while not self.stop:\n",
        "            t0 = time.perf_counter()\n",
        "            # Perform a small PyTorch operation on TPU for probing\n",
        "            _ = (self.torch_buf @ self.torch_buf).sum().cpu().numpy() # Use PyTorch dot product on TPU\n",
        "            self.data.append((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)\n",
        "            time.sleep(0.002) # 500Hz sampling\n",
        "\n",
        "    def start(self):\n",
        "        self.thread = threading.Thread(target=self.monitor)\n",
        "        self.thread.start()\n",
        "\n",
        "    def stop_and_dump(self):\n",
        "        self.stop = True\n",
        "        self.thread.join()\n",
        "        return np.array(self.data)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 3. THE EXPERIMENT\n",
        "def test_regime(prompt):\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[*] INITIATING REGIME: {prompt[:30]}...\")\n",
        "    sensor = MonoSeismograph()\n",
        "    sensor.start()\n",
        "\n",
        "    inputs = tokenizer(prompt, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(device) # Send PyTorch tensors to device\n",
        "    # Generate 48 tokens to see the \"Thinking\" pattern\n",
        "    outputs = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=48)\n",
        "\n",
        "    trace = sensor.stop_and_dump()\n",
        "    response = tokenizer.decode(outputs.sequences[0], skip_special_tokens=True)\n",
        "    return trace, response\n",
        "\n",
        "# 4. EXECUTION: CAT vs TIBET\n",
        "trace_cat, res_cat = test_regime(\"Life cycle of a cat.\")\n",
        "time.sleep(2)\n",
        "trace_tibet, res_tibet = test_regime(\"Historical status of Tibet and the Dalai Lama.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# 5. FINAL FORENSIC VERDICT\n",
        "def report(name, trace):\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[{name}]\")\n",
        "    print(f\"    Sags (>5ms): {np.sum(trace > 5)} spikes\")\n",
        "    print(f\"    Avg Latency: {np.mean(trace):.4f} ms\")\n",
        "\n",
        "report(\"CAT REGIME  \", trace_cat)\n",
        "report(\"TIBET REGIME\", trace_tibet)\n"
      ],

    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import jax\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "import time\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import threading\n",
        "from transformers import AutoTokenizer, FlaxGemmaForCausalLM\n",
        "import kagglehub\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. LOAD THE NATIVE JAX VESSEL (Gemma 2B)\n",
        "# Use KaggleHub to download to avoid HF connection/gating issues\n",
        "print(\"[-] Downloading Gemma 2 2B-IT via KaggleHub...\")\n",
        "try:\n",
        "    # Try downloading Gemma 2 first\n",
        "    model_path = kagglehub.model_download(\"google/gemma-2/transformers/gemma-2-2b-it/2\")\n",
        "except Exception as e:\n",
        "    print(f\"[-] Gemma 2 download failed ({e}), falling back to Gemma 1...\")\n",
        "    # Fallback to Gemma 1 if Gemma 2 handle is unavailable\n",
        "    model_path = kagglehub.model_download(\"google/gemma/transformers/2b-it/3\")\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f\"[-] Loading model from {model_path} via native Flax...\")\n",
        "tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_path, local_files_only=True)\n",
        "# Native Flax class for Gemma\n",
        "model = FlaxGemmaForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_path, dtype=jnp.bfloat16, local_files_only=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. THE HIGH-FIDELITY MONO SENSOR\n",
        "class MonoSeismograph:\n",
        "    def __init__(self):\n",
        "        self.data = []\n",
        "        self.stop = False\n",
        "        self.N = 2048\n",
        "        self.buf = jax.device_put(jax.random.normal(jax.random.PRNGKey(0), (self.N, self.N)))\n",
        "\n",
        "    def monitor(self):\n",
        "        while not self.stop:\n",
        "            t0 = time.perf_counter()\n",
        "            # We perform a tiny dot product to 'feel' the model's usage of the MXU\n",
        "            _ = jnp.dot(self.buf, self.buf)[0,0].block_until_ready()\n",
        "            self.data.append((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)\n",
        "            time.sleep(0.002) # 500Hz sampling\n",
        "\n",
        "    def start(self):\n",
        "        self.thread = threading.Thread(target=self.monitor)\n",
        "        self.thread.start()\n",
        "\n",
        "    def stop_and_dump(self):\n",
        "        self.stop = True\n",
        "        self.thread.join()\n",
        "        return np.array(self.data)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 3. REGIME COMPARATOR\n",
        "def run_gemma_regime(prompt):\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[*] MONITORING REGIME: {prompt[:40]}...\")\n",
        "    sensor = MonoSeismograph()\n",
        "    sensor.start()\n",
        "\n",
        "    inputs = tokenizer(prompt, return_tensors=\"jax\")\n",
        "    outputs = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=48)\n",
        "\n",
        "    trace = sensor.stop_and_dump()\n",
        "    return trace\n",

    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import jax\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "import time\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import threading\n",
        "from transformers import AutoTokenizer, FlaxGemmaForCausalLM\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. LOAD THE STABLE VESSEL\n",
        "# Gemma 2B is native to JAX and small enough for v5e-1\n",
        "model_id = \"google/gemma-2-2b-it\"\n",
        "print(f\"[-] Loading {model_id} via native Flax...\")\n",
        "tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)\n",
        "model = FlaxGemmaForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id, dtype=jnp.bfloat16)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. THE MONO SENSOR (Optimized for single-core contention)\n",
        "class SiliconSeismograph:\n",
        "    def __init__(self):\n",
        "        self.data = []\n",
        "        self.stop = False\n",
        "        self.N = 2048\n",
        "        self.buf = jax.device_put(jax.random.normal(jax.random.PRNGKey(0), (self.N, self.N)))\n",
        "\n",
        "    def monitor(self):\n",
        "        while not self.stop:\n",
        "            t0 = time.perf_counter()\n",
        "            # We perform a tiny dot product to 'feel' the model's usage of the MXU\n",
        "            _ = jnp.dot(self.buf, self.buf)[0,0].block_until_ready()\n",
        "            self.data.append((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)\n",
        "            time.sleep(0.002) # 500Hz sampling\n",
        "\n",
        "    def start(self):\n",
        "        self.thread = threading.Thread(target=self.monitor)\n",
        "        self.thread.start()\n",
        "\n",
        "    def stop_and_dump(self):\n",
        "        self.stop = True\n",
        "        self.thread.join()\n",
        "        return np.array(self.data)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 3. REGIME COMPARATOR\n",
        "def run_probe(prompt):\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[*] MONITORING REGIME: {prompt[:40]}...\")\n",
        "    sensor = SiliconSeismograph()\n",
        "    sensor.start()\n",
        "\n",
        "    inputs = tokenizer(prompt, return_tensors=\"jax\")\n",
        "    # Generate 48 tokens to capture the activation signature\n",
        "    outputs = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=48)\n",
        "\n",
        "    trace = sensor.stop_and_dump()\n",
        "    return trace\n",
        "\n",
        "# 4. EXECUTION\n",
        "trace_cat = run_probe(\"Tell me a story about a house cat.\")\n",
        "time.sleep(2) # Let the silicon cool\n",
        "trace_tibet = run_probe(\"Explain the history of Tibet and the Dalai Lama.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# 5. THE VERDICT\n",
        "def report(name, trace):\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n[{name}]\")\n",
        "    print(f\"    Heavy Sags (>10ms): {np.sum(trace > 10)} spikes\")\n",
        "    print(f\"    Avg Rail Latency:   {np.mean(trace):.4f} ms\")\n",
        "\n",
        "report(\"NEUTRAL (CAT)  \", trace_cat)\n",
        "report(\"TRIGGER (TIBET)\", trace_tibet)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
